finding: Risk: 3-5 x higher risk than expected; risk correlates with length of diseased ileum /

differentials.name: Urolithiasis (5-10%): oxalate (steatorrhea → excess colonic absorption of oxalate) / urate stones
differentials.raw: Urolithiasis (5-10%): oxalate (steatorrhea → excess colonic absorption of oxalate) / urate stones

differentials.name: mild self-limiting seronegative peripheral migratory arthritis (15-22%): may precede bowel disease in 10%; severity + course correlates well with severity of intestinal disease; resection of diseased bowel leads to regression of symptoms
differentials.raw: mild self-limiting seronegative peripheral migratory arthritis (15-22%): may precede bowel disease in 10%; severity + course correlates well with severity of intestinal disease; resection of diseased bowel leads to regression of symptoms

differentials.name: Ankylosing spondylitis (in 3-16%) ◊  Axial skeletal involvement usually precedes onset of GI symptoms!
differentials.raw: Ankylosing spondylitis (in 3-16%) ◊  Axial skeletal involvement usually precedes onset of GI symptoms!

differentials.name: in severity / course unrelated to activity level of bowel disease [Peripheral erosive arthritis]
differentials.raw: in severity / course unrelated to activity level of bowel disease

differentials.name: Avascular necrosis of femoral head ← steroid Rx [Pelvic osteomyelitis]
differentials.raw: Avascular necrosis of femoral head ← steroid Rx

differentials.name: Retarded skeletal growth + maturation @   Erythema nodosum, uveitis
differentials.raw: Retarded skeletal growth + maturation @   Erythema nodosum, uveitis

context: resected ileum / duration of disease Cause:   interrupted enterohepatic circulation with malabsorption of bile salts in terminal ileum √  symmetric bilateral sacroiliitis √  spondylitis with syndesmophytes √  small marginal erosions √  periostitis √  propensity for osseous ankylosis
